content: For creative-production pipelines with 9+ sequential phases, a mixed depth-2/depth-3 hierarchy is optimal. Phases with few specialists (≤3) work well at depth-2, while phases with many distinct sub-areas (5+ groups like worldbuilding's geography/magic/politics/culture/history, or beta-reading's 5 reader lenses) benefit from depth-3 sub-coordinators. This run used depth-2 for concept, style, revision, polish (3-4 specialists each) and depth-3 for worldbuilding, character, plotting, drafting, beta-reading (5-7 specialists each), producing 67 agents within the 80-agent budget at 85% utilization.
sourceEvidence: Depth decisions: concept-coordinator depth=2 (3 specialists, 1 group), worldbuilding-coordinator depth=3 (6 specialists, 5 groups), character-coordinator depth=3 (5 specialists, 3 groups, 4 cross-cutting), beta-reading-coordinator depth=3 (7 specialists, 5 groups). Budget: 68 of 80 used (85%).

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
